name: io.github.24X7/riz
title: riz
description: Self-hosted runtime that exposes your own HTTP/Lambda handlers as MCP tools over HTTP.

port.description: The local port riz is listening on (set via `riz run`; defaults to 3000).

io.modelcontextprotocol.registry/publish.summary: riz is not a fixed-tool MCP server. It is a single static Rust binary (~11 MB compressed download) you run on your own machine (Linux/macOS, x86_64 + aarch64). Each function declared in the user's riz.toml is auto-exposed as one MCP tool at /_riz/mcp the moment riz boots. The available tool set is therefore dynamic and user-defined, not a fixed list shipped by this package.
io.modelcontextprotocol.registry/publish.connect: claude mcp add riz-local --transport http http://localhost:3000/_riz/mcp
capabilities: Six runtimes in one binary: Bun, Node.js, Python, Rust, Go, capability-sandboxed WASM (WASI deny-by-default).
Every riz.toml function auto-registers as a typed MCP tool at /_riz/mcp (zero SDK glue).
The same route-table walk also serves a public OpenAPI 3.1 document at GET /openapi.json — every function is at once an MCP tool and an OpenAPI operation.

Host-enforced resource broker: sandboxed WASM reaches Postgres, DynamoDB, S3, and origin-pinned HTTP via capability grants — credentials and SigV4 signing stay host-side, no sockets/DSNs/keys in guest memory.
Built-in OpenAI-compatible LLM gateway on the same binary.
No per-request cold start (warm pre-spawned pool per function).
